rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF TERRY TINYEAH FANG
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. Distribution, however, will be ordered to be made to the trustee of the devisee trust, as the pour-over will directs. NB: In order for this Court to track closed matters, orders to terminate a proceeding must begin with the following language: "Order for Final Distribution." [LRSF 14.35 I.6.b.] Please make a note of it. Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SARAH ANNE LUM
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required, as the following issues (initially e-mailed to counsel on 07/10/2024, subsequently posted as the tentative ruling for the 09/30/2024 hearing, and last updated by this tentative ruling) remain unaddressed: 1) No original will has been filed. Note: The original will must be conventionally filed (cannot be e-filed). See LRSF 14.59(C)(1). 2) No proposed order has been received or lodged. 3) No DE-147 form is on file. 4) An amended DE-147S form must be filed for Shari Lum to state her correct middle initial at item 1. 5) No DE-121 form is on file. A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the form at least 15 days before the hearing. 6) The box was not checked in the petition caption to request IAEA authority. 7) Item 2d of the petition: As Shari Lum is not a California resident, a declaration required by LRSF 14.16(F) must be filed to state the total amount of anticipated liabilities of the decedent and claims against the estate and describe such liabilities and claims. A minimum bond ($20,000) will be required for Shari Lum regardless of a bond waiver provision in the will. See also Probate Code section 8571 & CRC 7.201(b). Note: An original bond must be conventionally filed (cannot be e-filed). See LRSF 14.59(C)(8). 8) Items 6f and 7a must be unchecked if the decedent was survived by issue. 9) Item 8: i) Are both petitioners the co-trustees and only beneficiaries of the devisee trust? ii) Please provide the date of death of Dwight Lum. iii) Are both petitioners Dwight Lum's children? iv) If Dwight Lum survived the decedent, his personal representative must be identified and receive notice. Please also provide a copy of letters issued to the personal representative. If there is no personal representative, each person who is entitled to succeed to Dwight Lum's estate must be identified and receive notice: A) If he died intestate, please identify all heirs including his surviving spouse or registered domestic partner if any. B) If he died testate, please identify all devisees named in his will. 10) The will refers to "Jennifer" Lum, but the petition was signed by "Jenifer" Lum. Please confirm the correct spelling of her first name. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF STEPHEN JOSEPH MCDONNELL
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/29/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Notice is defective if mailed before the filing of the pleading requiring notice. Although file stamped on 10/25/24, the amended petition was not e-filed until 10/28/24, when it was first available to be viewed on the Court's website. [LRSF 14.14 A.] (2) The amended petition is incomplete: no Attachment 4c (copy of the will) attached; no Attachment 7, setting forth the facts upon which the petitioner bases the allegation that the property should pass or be confirmed to her; and Attachment 9 does not show the relationships to the decedent or ages (age of a minor or "adult") of the persons listed. (3) Copies of bank statements immediately preceding the decedent's date of death must be filed to show the vesting of those accounts. Deadline is 1/15/25 for providing the assigned examiner courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE MARRIAGE OF MATTHEW BARNEY AND OLENA STAROSTINA
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION TO ESTABLISH FACT OF MARRIAGE
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. A verified supplement to be filed to clear the issues: (1) Petition does not allege why this petition is necessary other than the county clerk refused to issue the document. Need further information why the form was not acceptable by the county clerk. (2) Petition does not present sufficient supporting information or material for the court to establish the fact. Need more information to support the marriage. For example, when/where the ceremony took place. Photos. Who performed the ceremony? A written declaration from the person who performed it. Witnesses' statements describing the ceremony regarding the marriage or ceremony. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.
